projects.title: Bodega Auto-Responder — Desktop Automation
projects.shortDescription: Hybrid Electron/Node.js platform for multi-channel messaging automation (WhatsApp & Gmail). Featuring a distributed architecture and automated lifecycle management.
projects.fullDescription: A robust desktop application built on a hybrid architecture that orchestrates multiple automation subsystems. It utilizes Electron (v34) as the native container, with a Node.js/Express backend running as an independent child process (forking) to ensure UI fluidity. The system integrates whatsapp-web.js (Puppeteer-based) and Google Cloud APIs (Gmail) for proactive and reactive messaging. Includes a full dashboard in React for real-time monitoring and a secure auto-update system via GitHub Releases, following a strict Separation of Concerns (SoC) design.
features: Distributed architecture with Electron Main and Node.js Child Process
WhatsApp automation via QR-based session persistence (Puppeteer)
Official Gmail integration through Google Cloud OAuth2 and APIs
Real-time monitoring dashboard with Recharts and Radix UI
Automated release system with Electron Builder and GitHub Updates
Resilient service management (auto-recovery of backend processes)
Proactive vs. Reactive automation engine (Trigger-based logic)
Clean-code structure with strict Separation of Concerns (SoC)

projects.title: Interactive Financial Analysis Hub
projects.shortDescription: AI-driven professional loan simulator (Android/Web). Implements French Amortization system with high-performance visualizations and native mobile distribution.
projects.fullDescription: A professional-grade financial engine designed for Engineering Economics, built with cutting-edge technologies (React and Tailwind 4). The application provides precise simulations for Ordinary Annuities (French System) and features dynamic, synchronized data visualizations for interest and capital decomposition. Developed with an AI-augmented workflow, it stands as a cross-platform solution distributed as a native Android app via Capacitor 8, offering technical reports in PDF and real-time KPI analysis against FED risk-free rates.
features: AI-driven development process optimizing mathematical accuracy
Cross-platform distribution: Progressive Web App and Native Android (.apk)
Synchronized charts for real-time visualization of debt balances
Professional PDF reporting with jsPDF and Share API integration
Critical Analysis module comparing loans vs. FED 2026 projections
Mobile-first design with high-fidelity micro-animations (Framer Motion)
Precise financial engine calculating EITR and total financial costs
Educational pop-ups explaining formulas and financial metrics

projects.title: Nexus C.A. — ERP Digital Transformation
projects.shortDescription: End-to-end ERP solution solving 'islands of information' for a real-world distributor. Built with a decoupled React/TypeScript architecture.
projects.fullDescription: Nexus C.A. represents a strategic digital transformation for a distribution company, replacing fragmented processes with a unified Enterprise Resource Planning system. The project features a decoupled architecture using high-performance React patterns, custom hooks for business logic, and a strategic dashboard for real-time decision making. Implemented comprehensive modules for Finance, HR, and Operations, ensuring data consistency across the entire organization through advanced state management and TypeScript safety.
features: Unified strategic dashboard eliminating data fragmentation
Finance module: Automated budget tracking and fiscal reporting
HR module: Centralized employee lifecycle and payroll management
Operations module: Optimized logistics and inventory tracking
Granular Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for corporate security
Decoupled Container/Presentation architecture for scalability

projects.title: Maze Hunter — OOP & Patterns Lab
projects.shortDescription: Sophisticated maze-solving engine in Java/JavaFX. Research-focused project on SOLID principles and complex system state persistence.
projects.fullDescription: Maze Hunter is a technical study of high-level Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and SOLID principles. Built with Java 22 and JavaFX, it implements procedurally generated environments and a complex state persistence system using GSON for JSON serialization. The project showcases mastery of clean architecture, design patterns, and efficient asset management in a modular desktop environment.
features: Complex procedural generation of maze structures
Full persistence layer using GSON for JSON state serialization
Mastery of SOLID principles and design patterns
History and telemetry tracking via 'Anales' record system
Modular resource management for assets and audio
Advanced state-machine for game-loop control
